See PROXY_OPTIONS for more. - Token create/delete is implicit via transfers that increase the supply","errors":{"AddressEmptyCode(address)":[{"details":"There's no code at `target` (it is not a contract)."}],"ERC1967InvalidImplementation(address)":[{"details":"The `implementation` of the proxy is invalid."}],"ERC1967NonPayable()":[{"details":"An upgrade function sees `msg.value > 0` that may be lost."}],"FailedCall()":[{"details":"A call to an address target failed. The target may have reverted."}],"InvalidInitialization()":[{"details":"The contract is already initialized."}],"NotInitializing()":[{"details":"The contract is not initializing."}],"RequestKeyConflict(bool,uint40,uint40,bytes16,bytes16)":[{"details":"Occurs during a request where (`reqId` is reused and/or `seqNum` < expected) and the prior call had a different key (caller,seqNum,reqId) so replay does not occur as it otherwise could","params":{"isNewReqId":"true: `reqIdAct` is new; false: value was reused with a different (caller,seqNum)","reqIdAct":"Actual request id received","reqIdExp":"Expected request id, based upon cache lookup by (caller,seqNum); Zero-value if !`isNewReqId`","seqNumAct":"Actual sequence number received","seqNumExp":"Expected sequence number based upon caller"}}],"SeqNumGap(uint40,uint40,address)":[{"details":"Occurs when seq num `rcvd` > `expect`, possible causes: error from network/provider/client/reorg     - Invariant: `seqNum` must increment monotonically for a write call, see table above","params":{"caller":"Caller's address","seqNumAct":"Received sequence number","seqNumExp":"Expected sequence number"}}],"UUPSUnauthorizedCallContext()":[{"details":"The call is from an unauthorized context."}],"UUPSUnsupportedProxiableUUID(bytes32)":[{"details":"The storage `slot` is unsupported as a UUID."}]},"events":{"ApprovalForAll(address,address,bool)":{"details":"Emitted when `account` grants or revokes permission to `operator` to transfer their tokens, according to `approved`."},"Initialized(uint64)":{"details":"Triggered when the contract has been initialized or reinitialized."},"ReqAck(bytes16,address,uint40,(bytes16,uint16,uint16,uint16,uint40,uint40),bool)":{"details":"A Request Acknowledgement Event occurs at the end of each request (if no revert) and bridges the gaps between data sources, clients, lossy/unreliable protocols/providers/neworks, and the on-chain call outcome - Captures 3 categories of data for off-chain calls:     - Key inputs (indexed params) allow a tx search via either:         - PK1: reqId: Allows a tx search on a single field such as to fill a gap on replay         - PK2: (caller,seqNum): Allows a tx search to audit a sequence for a caller     - Context: `replay` and `callRes.blockNum` provide context on when/where this happened     - Output: `callRes` provides fields (rc, lrc, count) for either a simple result or summary         - Additional results can be found in the tx receipt logs from prior events in the same tx","params":{"callRes":"See `CallRes`; `reqId` and `ReqAck` are separate params for indexes","caller":"Account initiating the tx","replay":"false for an original event, true when `reqId` was already processed","reqId":"From CallRes: Request ID, allows for gap-fill on replay","seqNum":"From CallRes: Caller's sequence number incremented monotonically"}},"TransferBatch(address,address,address,uint256[],uint256[])":{"details":"Equivalent to multiple {TransferSingle} events, where `operator`, `from` and `to` are the same for all transfers."},"TransferSingle(address,address,address,uint256,uint256)":{"details":"Emitted when `value` amount of tokens of type `id` are transferred from `from` to `to` by `operator`."},"URI(string,uint256)":{"details":"Emitted when the URI for token type `id` changes to `value`, if it is a non-programmatic URI. If an {URI} event was emitted for `id`, the standard https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-1155#metadata-extensions[guarantees] that `value` will equal the value returned by {IERC1155MetadataURI-uri}."},"Upgraded(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the implementation is upgraded."}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"balanceOf(address,uint256)":{"params":{"account":"Ownership being inspected","id":"(tokenId) The security within the contract"},"returns":{"balance":"Account balance per (account,tokenId)"}},"balanceOfBatch(address[],uint256[])":{"params":{"accounts":"Ownership being inspected","ids":"(tokenIds) The securities within the contract"},"returns":{"balances":"Account balance per (account,tokenId)"}},"constructor":{"custom:api":"private","details":"Ensures the logic contract cannot be hijacked before the `initializer` runs - Sets version to `type(uint64).max` + `emit Initialized(version)` to prevent future initialization - `initialize` is where the business logic is initialized on proxies - For more info see comments in 'Initializable.sol'"},"getCallResByReqId(bytes16)":{"custom:api":"public","details":"Get a result for a prior write function call invoked from off-chain by this caller - Subject to storage lag when retrieved off-chain shortly after write, prefer the `ReqAck` event in that case","params":{"reqId":"Request ID of a prior call"},"returns":{"cr":"See `CallRes`"}},"getCallResBySeqNum(uint40)":{"custom:api":"public","details":"Get a result for a prior write function call invoked from off-chain by this caller - Subject to storage lag when retrieved off-chain shortly after write, prefer the `ReqAck` event in that case","params":{"seqNum":"Sequence number of a prior call by this caller"},"returns":{"cr":"See `CallRes`"}},"getContract(uint8)":{"details":"Get a contract address","params":{"contractId":"Contract ID requested"},"returns":{"_0":"Requested contract address"}},"getSeqNum(address)":{"details":"Get next expected seq num for the given account address","params":{"account":"Address to lookup"},"returns":{"seqNumEx":"Next expected seq num to be passed to a write function call from `account`"}},"getTokenIds(uint256,uint256)":{"details":"Get a slice of token IDs in circulation","params":{"count":"Items to get, 0 = [iBegin:] (may exceed gas), See PAGE_REQUESTS.","iBegin":"Index in the array to start processing"},"returns":{"tokenIds":"A slice of token ids in circulation"}},"getVersion()":{"details":"Get the current version"},"initialize(address,bytes16,string)":{"custom:api":"protected","details":"Basically replaces the constructor in a proxy oriented contract - `initializer` modifier ensures this function can only be called once during deploy - See UUPS_UPGRADE_SEQ for details on how to upgrade this contract","params":{"creator":"Creator's address for access control during setup","reqId":"Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers","url":"The generic URL to token metadata for all tokens"}},"isApprovedForAll(address,address)":{"details":"See `setApprovalForAll`, this reflects the governance Param `account` (arg 1) is omitted since it is ignored in the query, see `setApprovalForAll` notes param account Ignored as all accounts are subject to the same policies","params":{"spender":"See above"},"returns":{"_0":"bool Answer to the question"}},"preUpgrade(uint40,bytes16,bytes16)":{"details":"Stage values to use in the next call to `__authorizeUpgrade`","params":{"reqId":"Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers","reqIdStage":"Param to stage","seqNumEx":"=0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`"}},"proxiableUUID()":{"details":"Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier."},"safeBatchTransferFrom(address,address,uint256[],uint256[],bytes)":{"custom:api":"private","details":"Call does not invoke code in `to` wallet (no ROI for this token), so more 'safe'/efficient than spec - Does not call `onERC1155BatchReceived` on `to` wallet"},"safeBatchTransferFromEx(uint40,bytes16,address,address,uint256[],uint256[])":{"custom:api":"public","details":"Not in the ERC-1155 spec but mimics `safeBatchTransferFrom` + seq num tracking - Call does not invoke code in `to` wallet (no ROI for this token), so more 'safe'/efficient than spec - Does not call `onERC1155BatchReceived` on `to` wallet","params":{"reqId":"Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers","seqNumEx":"=0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`"}},"safeTransferFrom(address,address,uint256,uint256,bytes)":{"custom:api":"private","details":"Call does not invoke code in `to` wallet (no ROI for this token), so more 'safe'/efficient than spec   with respect to this contract, less safe for wallets incapable of handling the token but it can be reversed - Does not call `onERC1155Received` on `to` wallet"},"safeTransferFromEx(uint40,bytes16,address,address,uint256,uint256)":{"custom:api":"public","details":"Not in the ERC-1155 spec but mimics `safeTransferFrom` + seq num tracking - Call does not invoke code in `to` wallet (no ROI for this token), so more 'safe'/efficient than spec   with respect to this contract, less safe for wallets incapable of handling the token but it can be reversed - Does not call `onERC1155Received` on `to` wallet","params":{"reqId":"Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers","seqNumEx":"=0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`"}},"setApprovalForAll(address,bool)":{"custom:api":"private","details":"While this function often allows owner-to-owner transfers, here it is centrally controlled to enforce   regulator mandates such as resolving lost ownership issues and a good control location","params":{"approved":"Whether the account may transfer tokens","spender":"The account on which to grant/revoke the transfer permission"}},"setApprovalForAllEx(uint40,bytes16,address,bool)":{"custom:api":"public","details":"Not in the ERC-1155 spec but mimics `setApprovalForAll` + seq num tracking - While this function often allows owner-to-owner transfers, here it is centrally controlled to enforce   regulator mandates such as resolving lost ownership issues and a good control location","params":{"approved":"Whether the account may transfer tokens","reqId":"Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers","seqNumEx":"=0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`","spender":"The account on which to grant/revoke the transfer permission"}},"setContract(uint40,bytes16,uint8,address)":{"custom:api":"public","details":"Less size than separate setters","params":{"contractId":"Identifies the contract being set","newProxy":"The new proxy address","reqId":"Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers","seqNumEx":"=0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`"}},"setUri(uint40,bytes16,string)":{"custom:api":"public","details":"Set the metadata URL, counterpart of `uri()` in ERC-1155 spec","params":{"newUri":"Metadata URL","reqId":"Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers","seqNumEx":"=0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`"}},"supportsInterface(bytes4)":{"details":"Implements ERC-165 so that contracts/wallets can discover ERC-1155 compatibility"},"transferBatch(uint40,bytes16,uint8,address[],address[],uint256[],uint256[])":{"custom:api":"public","details":"Provides more flexibility and gas efficiency than ERC-1155 transfer functions","params":{"eventMode":"Controls event emission, `EventMode.PerXfer` is most compliant with downstream viewers     by following the ERC-1155 protocol for event consumers. A call to `balanceOf` works after all options","reqId":"Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers","seqNumEx":"=0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`"}},"upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes)":{"custom:oz-upgrades-unsafe-allow-reachable":"delegatecall","details":"Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event."},"uri(uint256)":{"details":"Set the uri/url for all tokenIds, such as https://token-cdn-domain/{id}.json - Clients must replace the literal '{id}' substring with the actual token id - See METADATA_FILE for more, like file content param id Ignored per the ERC-1155 standard as the 'id' is a placeholder in the result","returns":{"_0":"The same result for all inputs based upon https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-1155#metadata"}}},"title":"CRT: A channel revenue token","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"balanceOf(address,uint256)":{"notice":"Get the balance for the account,tokenId"},"balanceOfBatch(address[],uint256[])":{"notice":"Get the balance for each (account,tokenId), a batch version of `balanceOf`"},"getTokenCount()":{"notice":"Get the constructor.details: Ensures the logic contract cannot be hijacked before the `initializer` runs - Sets version to `type(uint64).max` + `emit Initialized(version)` to prevent future initialization - `initialize` is where the business logic is initialized on proxies - For more info see comments in 'Initializable.sol'

getCallResByReqId(bytes16).details: Get a result for a prior write function call invoked from off-chain by this caller - Subject to storage lag when retrieved off-chain shortly after write, prefer the `ReqAck` event in that case

params.reqId: Request ID of a prior call

getCallResBySeqNum(uint40).details: Get a result for a prior write function call invoked from off-chain by this caller - Subject to storage lag when retrieved off-chain shortly after write, prefer the `ReqAck` event in that case

params.seqNum: Sequence number of a prior call by this caller

getSeqNum(address).details: Get next expected seq num for the given account address

returns.seqNumEx: Next expected seq num to be passed to a write function call from `account`

getTokenIds(uint256,uint256).details: Get a slice of token IDs in circulation

params.count: Items to get, 0 = [iBegin:] (may exceed gas), See PAGE_REQUESTS.
params.iBegin: Index in the array to start processing

returns.tokenIds: A slice of token ids in circulation

initialize(address,bytes16,string).details: Basically replaces the constructor in a proxy oriented contract - `initializer` modifier ensures this function can only be called once during deploy - See UUPS_UPGRADE_SEQ for details on how to upgrade this contract

params.creator: Creator's address for access control during setup
params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.url: The generic URL to token metadata for all tokens

isApprovedForAll(address,address).details: See `setApprovalForAll`, this reflects the governance Param `account` (arg 1) is omitted since it is ignored in the query, see `setApprovalForAll` notes param account Ignored as all accounts are subject to the same policies

preUpgrade(uint40,bytes16,bytes16).details: Stage values to use in the next call to `__authorizeUpgrade`

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

safeBatchTransferFrom(address,address,ui.details: Call does not invoke code in `to` wallet (no ROI for this token), so more 'safe'/efficient than spec - Does not call `onERC1155BatchReceived` on `to` wallet

safeBatchTransferFromEx(uint40,bytes16,a.details: Not in the ERC-1155 spec but mimics `safeBatchTransferFrom` + seq num tracking - Call does not invoke code in `to` wallet (no ROI for this token), so more 'safe'/efficient than spec - Does not call `onERC1155BatchReceived` on `to` wallet

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

safeTransferFrom(address,address,uint256.details: Call does not invoke code in `to` wallet (no ROI for this token), so more 'safe'/efficient than spec   with respect to this contract, less safe for wallets incapable of handling the token but it can be reversed - Does not call `onERC1155Received` on `to` wallet

safeTransferFromEx(uint40,bytes16,addres.details: Not in the ERC-1155 spec but mimics `safeTransferFrom` + seq num tracking - Call does not invoke code in `to` wallet (no ROI for this token), so more 'safe'/efficient than spec   with respect to this contract, less safe for wallets incapable of handling the token but it can be reversed - Does not call `onERC1155Received` on `to` wallet

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

setApprovalForAll(address,bool).details: While this function often allows owner-to-owner transfers, here it is centrally controlled to enforce   regulator mandates such as resolving lost ownership issues and a good control location

params.approved: Whether the account may transfer tokens
params.spender: The account on which to grant/revoke the transfer permission

setApprovalForAllEx(uint40,bytes16,addre.details: Not in the ERC-1155 spec but mimics `setApprovalForAll` + seq num tracking - While this function often allows owner-to-owner transfers, here it is centrally controlled to enforce   regulator mandates such as resolving lost ownership issues and a good control location

params.approved: Whether the account may transfer tokens
params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`
params.spender: The account on which to grant/revoke the transfer permission

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

setUri(uint40,bytes16,string).details: Set the metadata URL, counterpart of `uri()` in ERC-1155 spec

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Implements ERC-165 so that contracts/wallets can discover ERC-1155 compatibility

transferBatch(uint40,bytes16,uint8,addre.details: Provides more flexibility and gas efficiency than ERC-1155 transfer functions

params.eventMode: Controls event emission, `EventMode.PerXfer` is most compliant with downstream viewers     by following the ERC-1155 protocol for event consumers. A call to `balanceOf` works after all options
params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

uri(uint256).details: Set the uri/url for all tokenIds, such as https://token-cdn-domain/{id}.json - Clients must replace the literal '{id}' substring with the actual token id - See METADATA_FILE for more, like file content param id Ignored per the ERC-1155 standard as the 'id' is a placeholder in the result

returns._0: The same result for all inputs based upon https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-1155#metadata

balanceOf(address,uint256).notice: Get the balance for the account,tokenId

balanceOfBatch(address[],uint256[]).notice: Get the balance for each (account,tokenId), a batch version of `balanceOf`

getTokenCount().notice: Get the count of token ids with a supply

getTokenSupply(uint256).notice: Get a token's supply (units in circulation)

isApprovedForAll(address,address).notice: Whether `spender` may direct tokens owned by `account`?

safeBatchTransferFrom(address,address,ui.notice: Xfer tokens `from` sender `to` recipient for each pair in: token `id` and `value` - This is an awkward func in the ERC-1155 spec since neither `from` nor `to` are arrays

safeBatchTransferFromEx(uint40,bytes16,a.notice: Xfer tokens `from` sender `to` recipient for each pair in: token `id` and `value` - This is an awkward func in the ERC-1155 spec since neither `from` nor `to` are arrays

safeTransferFrom(address,address,uint256.notice: Xfer a single token `from` sender `to` recipient for token `id` and `value`

safeTransferFromEx(uint40,bytes16,addres.notice: Xfer a single token `from` sender `to` recipient for token `id` and `value`

setApprovalForAll(address,bool).notice: Grants/revokes an account approval for transfering all tokens

setApprovalForAllEx(uint40,bytes16,addre.notice: Grants/revokes an account approval for transfering all tokens
